name: Face
summary: Faces in SketchUp are flat, 2-sided polygons with 3 or more sides.

methods.name: all_connected
methods.summary: The all_connected method retrieves all of the entities connected to a face.
methods.remarks: The all_connected method retrieves all of the entities connected to a face.

returns.doc: the entities connected to the face

methods.name: area
methods.summary: The area method is used to retrieve the area of a face.
methods.remarks: The area method is used to retrieve the area of a face. You can pass in an optional Transformation (or an array that can represent a transformation), to correct for a parent group's transformation. For example, if a face is inside of a group that is scaled to 200%, the area method will return the unscaled area of the face. So by passing a 200% transformation object to this method, you can account for that to get the “visual” area of the face.

params.name: transform
params.doc: A Transformation object or array that can be interpreted as a Transformation object.

returns.doc: the area of the face in square inches.

methods.name: classify_point
methods.summary: The classify_point method is used to determine if a given Point3d is on the referenced Face.
methods.remarks: The classify_point method is used to determine if a given Point3d is on the referenced Face. It is important that return value comparisons be made against the symbolic constants (i.e. PointUnknown, PointInside, PointOnVertex, etc.) rather than the absolute integer values as these values may change from one release to the next.

params.name: point
params.doc: A Point3d.

returns.doc: an integer describing where a Point3d is in relation to the referenced Face.

methods.name: clear_texture_position
methods.summary: The #clear_texture_position method is used to remove any explicit texture positioning for a face and have SketchUp display it with the default texture positioning.
methods.remarks: The #clear_texture_position method is used to remove any explicit texture positioning for a face and have SketchUp display it with the default texture positioning.

params.name: front
params.doc: true Clears on the front side of the face, false the back side.

methods.name: clear_texture_projection
methods.summary: The #clear_texture_projection method is used to clear the texture projection.
methods.remarks: The #clear_texture_projection method is used to clear the texture projection. This is similar to toggling off Projection from the Position Texture tool in the UI.

params.name: frontside
params.doc: true for front side, false for back side.

methods.name: coplanar_with?
methods.summary: The #coplanar_with? method is used determine whether a face is coplanar with `other_face`.
methods.remarks: The #coplanar_with? method is used determine whether a face is coplanar with `other_face`.

params.name: other_face
params.doc: The face to compare with.

methods.name: edges
methods.summary: The edges method is used to get an array of edges that bound the face.
methods.remarks: The edges method is used to get an array of edges that bound the face.

returns.doc: an array of Edge objects (if successful)

methods.name: followme
methods.summary: The #followme method is used to create a shape by making the face follow along an array of edges.
methods.remarks: The #followme method is used to create a shape by making the face follow along an array of edges.

params.name: edges
params.doc: An array of edge objects to follow.

methods.name: get_glued_instances
methods.summary: The get_glued_instances method returns an Array any ComponentInstances that are glued to the face.
methods.remarks: The get_glued_instances method returns an Array any ComponentInstances that are glued to the face.

returns.doc: An array of ComponentInstance objects that are currently glued to the face.

methods.name: get_texture_projection
methods.summary: The #get_texture_projection method will return a vector representing the projection for either the front or back side of the face.
methods.remarks: The #get_texture_projection method will return a vector representing the projection for either the front or back side of the face.

params.name: frontside
params.doc: true for front side, false for back side.

returns.doc: a vector on success, nil if face is not textured with a projected texture mapping.

methods.name: get_UVHelper
methods.signature: get_UVHelper(front = true, back = true) => Sketchup::UVHelper | get_UVHelper(front = true, back = true, texturewriter) => Sketchup::UVHelper
methods.summary: The get_UVHelper object is used to retrieve a UVHelper object for use in texture manipulation on a face.
methods.remarks: The get_UVHelper object is used to retrieve a UVHelper object for use in texture manipulation on a face.

params.name: front
params.doc: True if you want the texture coordinates for the front face, false if not.

params.name: back
params.doc: True if you want the texture coordinates for the back face, false if not.
params.default: true) => Sketchup::UVHelper | get_UVHelper(front = true, back = true, texturewriter

methods.name: loops
methods.summary: The loops method is used to get an array of all of the loops that bound the face.
methods.remarks: The loops method is used to get an array of all of the loops that bound the face.

returns.doc: an array of Loop objects if successful

methods.name: mesh
methods.summary: The mesh method creates a polygon mesh that represents the face.
methods.remarks: The mesh method creates a polygon mesh that represents the face. See the Geom::PolygonMesh class for more information. Valid flags are: 0: Include PolygonMeshPoints, 1: Include PolygonMeshUVQFront, 2: Include PolygonMeshUVQBack, 4: Include PolygonMeshNormals. Use bitwise OR to combine flags. A value of 7 will include all flags, for example.

params.name: flags
params.doc: One or more flags used to generate a mesh.

methods.name: normal
methods.summary: The normal method is used to retrieve the 3D vector normal to the face in the front direction.
methods.remarks: The normal method is used to retrieve the 3D vector normal to the face in the front direction.

methods.name: outer_loop
methods.summary: This method is used to retrieve the outer loop that bounds the face.
methods.remarks: This method is used to retrieve the outer loop that bounds the face.

returns.doc: a Loop object representing the outer loop (if successful)

methods.name: plane
methods.summary: The plane method is used to retrieve the plane of the face.
methods.remarks: The plane method is used to retrieve the plane of the face. See the Array class for information on how planes are stored.

returns.doc: a plane that contains the face (if successful)

methods.name: position_material
methods.signature: position_material(material, points, on_front) => Sketchup::Face, false | position_material(material, points, on_front, projection) => Sketchup::Face, false
methods.summary: The #position_material method is used to position a material on a face.
methods.remarks: The #position_material method is used to position a material on a face. The points argument must contain 2, 4, 6 or 8 points. The points are used in pairs to describe where a point in the texture image is positioned on the Face. The first point in each pair is a 3D point in the model. The second point in each pair of points is a 2D point that gives the (u,v) coordinates of a point in the image to match up with the 3D point.

params.name: points
params.doc: An array of Point3d objects used to position the material. The points should be on the plane of the face. If they are not they will be projected to the face's plane.

params.name: on_front
params.doc: true to position the texture on the front of the Face or false to position it on the back of the Face.

returns.doc: the face upon success, false if material or texture if not valid.

methods.name: pushpull
methods.summary: The pushpull method is used to perform a push/pull on a face.
methods.remarks: The pushpull method is used to perform a push/pull on a face. The distance is measured in the direction that the face normal is pointing.

params.name: distance
params.doc: The distance to push/pull the face.

params.name: copy
params.doc: Create a new push/pull starting face if true (equivalent of pressing CTRL while in SketchUp), do not create a push/pull starting face if false.

methods.name: reverse!
methods.summary: The reverse! method is used to reverse the face's orientation, meaning the front becomes the back.
methods.remarks: The reverse! method is used to reverse the face's orientation, meaning the front becomes the back.

returns.doc: the reversed Face object if successful, false if unsuccessful

methods.name: set_texture_projection
methods.summary: Deprecated.
methods.remarks: Deprecated. This function never worked correctly. It's not capable of controlling the position and orientation of the texture. In some cases it produced an invalid model. As of SketchUp 2021.1 the method simply raises NotImplementedError. The #set_texture_projection method is used to set the texture projection direction.

params.name: vector
params.doc: representing the direction of the projection. Use nil to remove the projection.

params.name: frontside
params.doc: true for front side, false for back side.

methods.name: texture_positioned?
methods.summary: The #texture_positioned? method is used to check if the face has a texture that is positioned.
methods.remarks: The #texture_positioned? method is used to check if the face has a texture that is positioned. A texture is positioned when it's not using the default texture coordinates. When a user uses the Paint Bucket Tool to apply a material sampled from the Material Browser it will use default texture coordinates and not be positioned. It will be positioned if the user uses the Position Texture Tool. When an API user uses Drawinglement#material= the texture is not positioned. It will be positioned when the API user uses #position_material. It it also positioned of the face was crafted via Geom::PolygonMesh.

params.name: front
params.doc: true Checks the front side of the face, false the back side.

methods.name: texture_projected?
methods.summary: The #texture_projected? method is used to check if the face has a texture that is projected.
methods.remarks: The #texture_projected? method is used to check if the face has a texture that is projected. A texture is projected when the user enables this property via the Position Texture Tool. It is also projected when the API user passes a projection vector to #position_material.

params.name: front
params.doc: true Checks the front side of the face, false the back side.

methods.name: uv_tile_at
methods.summary: The #uv_tile_at method is used to get the corner positions (model and UV) of a UV tile.
methods.remarks: The #uv_tile_at method is used to get the corner positions (model and UV) of a UV tile. The UV tile bounds the given reference point on the plane of the face. If the reference isn't on the plane of the face it will be projected onto it. The world coordinates are on the plane of the face unless the texture is projected. When the texture is projected the the world points are on an arbitrary plane that is perpendicular to the projection direction. The returned coordinates are arranged to be compatible with #position_material. Getting the bounds of the UV tile under the cursor: The red quadrilateral represents the model points returned.

params.name: position
params.doc: Model position on the face's plane that will be bounded by the UV tile.

params.name: front
params.doc: true Checks the front side of the face, false the back side.

returns.doc: A set of 8 points. Each stride of two is first a model space point, the second a UV coordinate. nil if the face doesn't have a texture on the given side.

methods.name: vertices
methods.summary: The vertices method is used to get an array of all of the vertices that bound the face.
methods.remarks: The vertices method is used to get an array of all of the vertices that bound the face.

returns.doc: an array of Vertex objects if successful

properties.name: back_material
properties.summary: The back_material method is used to retrieve the material assigned to the back side of the face.
properties.remarks: The back_material method is used to retrieve the material assigned to the back side of the face.

properties.name: material
properties.summary: The material method is used to retrieve the material assigned to the front of the face.
properties.remarks: The material method is used to retrieve the material assigned to the front of the face. (This method is inherited from the Drawingelement parent class.)
